It ranges from verbal abuse to physical harm: Eight years ago a U.S Marine punched her in the face. There also are threats from the homeless people who sometimes inhabit the park. Park police are another problem, since sleeping is considered camping: -- a park offense -- and leaving posters unattended also is unlawful. Mrs. Picciotto and Mr. Thomas frequently play 'cat-and-mouse' with their adversaries. Sometimes they lose. Last summer Mr. Thomas, who calls himself an intellectual and spends a lot of time in the library, served 90 days in the pokey for camping. THERE ARE good moments. too. Every week a woman from the Humane Society delivers a bag of peanuts to Mrs. Picciotto so she can feed her beloved squirrels. She sometimes receives mail from people she has become friends with. (The Vigil has a post office box). And after her bike was stolen, a young man employed at a nearby bicycle shop pieced together another for her Despite the hardships. the sidewalk across from the White House will remain Mrs. Picciotto's home for the foreseeable future. texts.full_text: ) to these neuropeptides exist in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) of living AD patients, using an enzyme immunoassay for their detection and quantification: - Methods: CSF samples were collected from 32 neurologically healthy controls and 48 AD patients. The levels of galanin and α-MSH autoAbs were measured using a commercially available enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) kit, following the manufacturer's instructions. Demographic data, cognitive assessments, and neuroimaging results were also recorded for statistical analysis. - Results: AD patients had significantly higher levels of galanin autoAb ( < 0.01), but not α-MSH autoAbs when compared to controls (Figs. 1and 2, respectively). In addition, a significant negative correlation was observed between the level of anti-galanin Abs and Mini Mental State Examination scores in AD patients ![Galanin AutoAb Levels]('figure1') *Figure 1: Galanin autoantibody levels in controls (n=32) vs Alzheimer's disease patients (n=48). Bars represent mean ± SD.Figure 2. α - MSH auto antibody levels were comparable between the two groups and did not correlate with cognitive impairment in AD subjects ( ).* This study provides evidence for dysregulation of immune response to galanin, which could play a role or be an epiphenomenon associated with Alzheimer's pathogenesis. The significance α - MSH autoAb levels remains unclear at this stage and further studies are needed...

texts.full_text: take a house out of a bay?” For thirty years her parent’s home sat on the small finger of land between Barnegat Bay and the Atlantic Ocean in the borough of Mantoloking. When Sandy hit and the barrier island was breached, Jackie’s house was washed off terra firma and into the bay. And there it sat for eight months, eerily intact just like that roller coaster, standing not so firm and raising many strange questions. (Picture: The house was washed into the bay during Superstorm Sandy and sat there for eight months before it was finally torn down and carted away via barge.) Natural disasters also lead to some strange emotions. There’s fear of course, some anger, a sense of loss no doubt and on darker days maybe even a sense of hopelessness. But to my surprise, even on the darkest day one can feel a “sense of relief” – so Jackie told me as we watched a crew tear down her family home, taking it from the bay and from her family forever. We'll feature a video showing Jackie's story—and more like hers—in the weeks to come, before the premiere of the new season of This Old House. texts.full_text: reak down CSRC and SSRC in the context of Real-Time Transport Protocol (RTP). SSRC (Synchronization Source): The SSRC field is a unique identifier for each RTP stream. It helps to identify where an RTP packet originates from within a multicast network or any scenario with multiple senders contributing data packets. In essence, when you have several sources sending data over the same channel in a multicast environment (or similar setup), SSRCs help distinguish between different streams of data coming from these various sources. Each stream is assigned its own unique SSRC value to ensure that no confusion arises about which source's packet it is during processing. CSRC (Contributing Source): The CSRC field, on the other hand, identifies additional sources contributing to a single RTP packet or sequence of packets in certain scenarios where multiple senders are involved. texts.full_text: onmental, geographic, and/or social factors. Bone samples from 25 middle Neolithic human individuals at seven archeological sites and comparative faunal samples were analyzed, and compared with contemporary series in Mediterranean Europe. Carbon and nitrogen isotope ratios (δ13 C and δ15 N) of bone collagen were studied to determine the dietary patterns. Dietary habits proved to be similar between communities, apart from some interpopulational variations in subsistence strategies. Their diet was based on C3 terrestrial resources with a major vegetal protein component. The reported variations in interpopulational subsitence strategies were not related to environmental, geographic, and/or social factors, which suggests a strong homogeneity in the diet of the Neolithic communities studied. This study contributes to a better understanding of the subsistence patterns and dietary habits of Neolithic communities in north-east Iberia, filling a gap in the knowledge of the region's prehistory. The findings provide valuable insights into how these ancient communities interacted with their environment and the resources available to them, offering a glimpse into their daily lives and survival strategies. Further research could expand on this study by analyzing more sites and comparing the results with other regions in Europe, potentially shedding light on the broader patterns and differences in Neolithic subsistence strategies across the continent. Additionally, integrating other lines of evidence, such as archaeobotanical data and artifact analysis, could offer a more comprehensive picture of the subsistence practices and economic activities of these ancient communities. In conclusion, this research underscores the importance of studying paleodietary reconstruction through stable isotopes as a tool for understanding past human behavior and adaptation to environmental and social challenges. texts.full_text: NEWS TOP 500 SHOP IR EVENTS Next 12/11/12 Fab.com leads the pack in social commerce The retailer has the top score among retail brands in a new report. Facebook Twitter Email Zak Stambor Fab.com has gone a long way toward solving the riddle of making money from social media. 24% of the flash-sale retailer’s traffic comes from shoppers clicking directly from social networks and half of its new customer acquisition stems from shoppers sharing the items they are interested in buying, or have bought. What’s more, the e-retailer reports a 15% conversion rate from what it calls socially discovered items, which are products that a shopper sees on a social network after one of her friends shared it. Those robust results are what makes the flash-sale retailer stand out among online retailers, according to a new report from social commerce technology company 8thBridge Inc. The report examined the 475 retailers in Internet Retailer’s Top 500 and Second 500 guides, as well as a handful ofother leading e-retailers. Fab.com's success can be attributed partly due to its emphasis on visual content such as product images that are share-worthy. It also uses storytelling techniques through product descriptions to engage shoppers emotionally with products

The student should plan the presentation in accordance with normal college hours. the total time of the presentation is 85 minutes. − The presentation and questions from the audience take about 45 minutes. − After the presentation the supervisors retreat for a private discussion with the student and a deliberation about the grade. This takes about 30 minutes. − The graduation ceremony takes about 10 minutes. During the graduation ceremony, the grade is announced and the student receives his/her diploma. In case the student did not finish all the courses before delivering the presentation and/or has not had a meeting with the Board of Examiners, the student will not receive the diploma immediately after the presentation. The student can collect the diploma after all the courses are completed and the records have been discussed by the board. ... texts.full_text: you please include the Android SDK and permissions required for implementing Speech Recognition? This will greatly assist someone who is new or unfamiliar with this process. Thank You! To run voice-to-text (Speech to Text) on anAndroid device, follow these steps: 1.Choose your project inAndoid Studio and open the build.gradle file underapp directory. 2.Add a dependency for Google's SpeechRecognizer: ```java dependencies { implementation 'com.google.android.gms:play-services-speech-recognition-android:x.x' } 3. Request Permissions: To use speech recognition, you'll need to request appropriate permissions in your AndroidManifest.xml. 4.Set up the Activity that will handle voice input and configure a SpeechRecognizer object. 5.Once done with setting it all-up call startListening() method on our recognizer instance which starts listening for audio from microphone. 6.The result of speech recognition is returned by callingonResults(). This returns an Bundleobject containing various information about recognized words, confidence level etc. 7.Finally don't forget to stop the SpeechRecognizer when done with it usingstopListener() Remember that this code will need some tweaking based on your specific needs and environment. It's a good idea for beginners in Android development or speech recognition programming who are looking into how voice-to-text works specifically within anAndroid device.

texts.full_text: common in Slovakia and the Dominican Republic than in countries.[2][3] Alkaptonuria is often asymptomatic, but the sclera of the eyes may be pigmented (often only at a later age),[1] and the skin may be darkened in sun-exposed areas and around sweat glands; sweat may be coloured brown. Urine may turn brown or even inky black if collected and left exposed to open air, especially when left standing for a period of time. Kidney stones and stone formation in the prostate (in men) are common and may occur in more than a quarter of cases.[1] The main symptoms of alkaptonuria are due to the accumulation of homogentisic acid in tissues. In the joints this leads to cartilage damage, specifically in the spine, leading to low back pain at a young age in most cases. Cartilage damage may also occur in the hip and shoulder. Joint replacement surgery (hip and shoulder) is often necessary at a relatively young age.[1] Intervertebral discs Calcification Valvular heart disease, mainly calcification and regurgitation of the aortic and mitral valves, may occur, and in severe and progressive cases valve replacement may be necessary. Coronary artery disease may be accelerated in alkaptonuria.[1] A distinctive characteristic of alkaptonuria is that ear wax exposed to air turns red or black (depending on diet) after hours because of the accumulation of homogentisic acid.[4] The diagnosis of alkaptonuria needs to be suspected before diagnostic testing can be performed using paper chromatography and thin layer chromatography. Both blood plasma and urine can be used for diagnosis. In healthy subjects, homogentisic acid is absent in both blood plasma and urine. texts.full_text: of microgels controlled by counterion clouds. Concentrated poly(N-isopropylacrylamide) (pNIPAM) microgel suspensions at a fixed temperature below the deswelling transition of pNIPAM exhibit spontaneous particle deswelling. The microgels deswell before they are in direct contact and in polydisperse suspensions this deswelling is most pronounced for the largest microgel particles; as a consequence, the polydispersity of the suspension is reduced. Recently, we presented a model for this spontaneous deswelling, which is based on the concept of counterion clouds. According to this model, the microgel particles are surrounded by a cloud of counterions that are attracted to the charged microgel surface. The counterion cloud is characterized by a certain thickness, which is determined by the electrostatic interactions between the counterions and the microgel surface. As the microgel particles approach each other, the counterion clouds overlap, leading to a decrease in the electrostatic repulsion between the particles. This symmetry implies that the odd moments of a zero-mean normal distribution are zero. Therefore, \[ \mathbb{E}(Y^n) = 0 \quad \text{for odd } n. \] ### Case 2: Even $n$ For even $n$, let's denote $n = 2k$ where $k$ is a positive integer. texts.full_text: g one's own advocate for health is a crucial aspect that empowers individuals to take charge of their well-being and navigate through various healthcare systems effectively. It involves being proactive, informed, assertive yet respectful in order ensure best possible care outcomes while maintaining dignity throughout entire process Here are some key elements I believe should be emphasized during your talk: 1. Knowledge is Power - Educating oneself about health conditions empowers individuals to make better decisions regarding treatment options and understand potential risks or side effects associated with different procedures/medications etc.. 2. Communication Skills Matter- Effective communication plays an integral role when advocating for one's own healthcare needs; being able express concerns clearly yet respectfully ensures that medical professionals listen attentively while taking patient seriously 3 . Assertiveness Without Aggression - Finding balance between assertiveness (standing up firmly) and aggression(being overly aggressive/confrontational ) is essential aspect of self advocacy. It involves speaking confidently but politely, asking questions persistently without being pushy or demanding 4.. Building Trustworthy Relationships With Healthcare Providers- Establishing trustful relationships with doctors , nurses etc helps create an environment where patients feel comfortable sharing personal information which ultimately leads better diagnosis/treatment plans tailored specifically towards individual needs 5 . Knowing Your Rights - Understanding legal rights pertaining medical care such as privacy laws, informed consent policies among others enables individuals protect themselves against any potential abuse or mistreatment during treatment process 6.. Support Networks Matter Too! Having supportive network comprising friends , family members etc provides emotional backing needed especially when dealing complex illnesses like breast cancer 7 . Staying Informed About Latest Developments - Keeping abreast latest advancements within medical field ensures patients receive most up-to date treatments available thereby increasing chances successful recovery outcomes These points highlight importance advocating actively towards personal healthcare needs while maintaining respectful attitude
